title: Build failing - Golang 1.9.4
body:
Following the install.md instructions, the build appears to be failing with:

```
make BUILDTAGS=""
touch .gopathok
go generate ./cmd/podman/varlink/...
../../../vendor/github.com/varlink/go/cmd/varlink-go-interface-generator/main.go:12:2: cannot find package "github.com/varlink/go/varlink/idl" in any of:
        /usr/lib/go-1.9/src/github.com/varlink/go/varlink/idl (from $GOROOT)
        /root/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/varlink/go/varlink/idl (from$GOPATH)
cmd/podman/varlink/generate.go:3: running "go": exit status 1
Makefile:274: recipe for target 'cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go' failed
make: *** [cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go] Error 1
```


I've tried make install.tools and it pulls certain projects, but not varlink
```
$ ls /root/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/
alecthomas  cpuguy83  projectatomic  sirupsen  vbatts
```

comments.body: Doesn't seem to exist in the makefile
Are you working off of master?

Sorry, I was looking at the wrong error. Here is the correct error:

```
$ make varlink_generate
touch .gopathok
go generate ./cmd/podman/varlink/...
../../../vendor/github.com/varlink/go/cmd/varlink-go-interface-generator/main.go:12:2: cannot find package "github.com/varlink/go/varlink/idl" in any of:
        /usr/lib/go-1.9/src/github.com/varlink/go/varlink/idl (from $GOROOT)
        /home/packer/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/varlink/go/varlink/idl (from $GOPATH)
cmd/podman/varlink/generate.go:3: running "go": exit status 1
Makefile:274: recipe for target 'cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go' failed
make: *** [cmd/podman/varlink/ioprojectatomicpodman.go] Error 1
```

Did you do a vndr command?  Currently the vndr command has a nasty habit of removing 
vendor/github.com/varlink

Try 
git checkout vendor/github.com/varlink
And the try the make line again.

I had an incorrect GOPATH configured.

Thanks for the help, sorry for the distraction.
Just happy there was not a bug.
